purpose: Make the two-truths structure explicit in the KuuOS OS bootstrap layer by separating conventional runtime operation from ultimate non-authority boundary conditions.

conventional_truth_surface.description: The runnable, inspectable, auditable local OS surface.
allowed_claims: a validation returned PASS or HOLD
the local bootstrap host completed a bounded cycle

ultimate_truth_boundary.description: The non-reifying boundary that prevents runtime outputs from becoming self-authorizing truth or action authority.
non_authority_clauses: validation is not truth by itself
audit receipt is not external audit acceptance
kernel state is not ultimate truth
module PASS is not execution authority
state checkpoint is not semantic memory overwrite
scheduler selection is not action permission
local autonomy is not unrestricted agency

gap_operator.name: two_truths_os_gap
gap_operator.role: Maintain a non-collapse gap between conventional runtime success and ultimate authority claims.

os_design_consequence.kernel_role: boundary operator rather than sovereign authority
os_design_consequence.scheduler_role: conditioned placement rather than command sovereignty
os_design_consequence.hold_role: middle-way safety state rather than mere failure
os_design_consequence.repair_role: candidate-only continuation rather than direct correction

required_invariants: conventional runtime remains useful and inspectable
runtime success and authority claims remain separated
state checkpoint cannot overwrite semantic memory
audit receipt cannot substitute for external acceptance
HOLD is a valid safety-preserving state
future kernel implementations must preserve this two-truths gap
